_meta.description: Bryan Johnson quotes and positions on aging interventions.
_meta.generation_method: Generated via gpt-5.2 with web search (OpenAI Responses API).
_meta.note: Quotes sourced via web search. Check 'verified' field — true means close to verbatim from a found source, false means paraphrased. URLs link to original sources where found.

quotes.text: On September 28th, I decided to stop rapamycin, ending almost 5 years of experimentation with this molecule for its longevity potential.
quotes.source: Blueprint Protocol blog post ('I stopped taking rapamycin')

quotes.text: I have tested various rapamycin protocols including weekly (5, 6, and 10 mg dose schedules), biweekly (13 mg), and alternating weekly (6/13 mg) to optimize rejuvenation and limit side effects.
quotes.source: Blueprint Protocol blog post ('I stopped taking rapamycin')

quotes.text: Despite the immense potential from pre-clinical trials, my team and I came to the conclusion that the benefits of lifelong dosing of Rapamycin do not justify the hefty side-effects (intermittent skin/soft tissue infections, lipid abnormalities, glucose elevations, and increased resting heart rate).
quotes.source: Blueprint Protocol blog post ('I stopped taking rapamycin')

quotes.text: With no other underlying causes identified, we suspected Rapamycin, and since dosage adjustments had no effect, we decided to discontinue it entirely.
quotes.source: Blueprint Protocol blog post ('I stopped taking rapamycin')

quotes.text: Every two weeks, I take 13 mg of gastro protected Rapamune (Rx required). Rapamycin is used to prevent organ transplant rejection and has possible anti-aging effects.
quotes.source: Archived X/Twitter post via posts.bryanjohnson.com

quotes.text: In September I stopped Rapamycin, based on concerns around its metabolic side-effects observed in my lab results (including increased cholesterol, blood sugar and RHR) …
quotes.source: Archived X/Twitter index excerpt via posts.bryanjohnson.com (page listing)

rapamycin.dosage_mentioned: Tested weekly 5 mg, 6 mg, 10 mg; biweekly 13 mg; alternating weekly 6/13 mg; and stated use of 13 mg every two weeks (gastro-protected Rapamune).
rapamycin.summary: He previously experimented with rapamycin for nearly five years (including 13 mg biweekly and other schedules) but stopped on Sept 28, 2024, concluding the side effects and metabolic changes outweighed the benefits for lifelong use and it is not in his current Blueprint protocol.

quotes.text:
I paused Metformin.
A longevity staple in my protocol for 5+ years.
But its effects on my body were shocking.

It built up my energy capacity,
but limited my ability to use 90% of it.

Here’s what we found.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com mirror of X/Twitter post (thread opener)

quotes.text: Yeah, the major pillars we've got diet, the pills, we've got the prescriptions, which is rapamycin, metformin, and carbos…
quotes.source: Ben Greenfield Life podcast transcript (Bryan Johnson interview)

quotes.text: Every single supplement has to justify its existence. It has to fight for its life. And everything needs to be measured. … But then, others like metformin, rapamycin, and carbos, everything has a purpose…
quotes.source: Ben Greenfield Life podcast transcript (Bryan Johnson interview)

quotes.text: Metformin 500 mg (Rx) (6 week cycles on and off)
quotes.source: Blueprint 'Bryan Johnson’s Protocol' page (Rx stack listing)

metformin.dosage_mentioned: 500 mg (Rx), 6 week cycles on and off
metformin.summary: Johnson treats metformin as a measured longevity intervention—after pausing it due to adverse performance effects, he lists it again in his current Blueprint Rx stack as a 500 mg cyclic protocol.

quotes.text: NR vs NMN? I used each for 90 days & both were effective in getting my intracellular NAD levels to age 16 equivalent, 31 yr age reversal as semi-indirect bioage marker. Recent levels 54.6μM via Jinfiniti test. Used @truniagen NR & @JinfinitiPM NMN.
quotes.source: X post (mirror), Apr 26, 2023

quotes.text: NR (450 mg) or NMN (500 mg)
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page, updated Jan 23, 2026

NMN.summary: He has self-experimented with NMN vs NR and reports both effectively raised his measured intracellular NAD, and his current Blueprint protocol lists NR (450 mg) or NMN (500 mg) as an active either/or option.

quotes.text: NR (450 mg) or NMN (500 mg)
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson’s Protocol'), Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: I trialed both. I did 90 days on NR, I did 90 days on NMN and I measured my intracellular blood levels throughout and I showed that both were basically effective… So I was able to peg my intracellular nad at the 18-year-old mark on both supplements.
quotes.source: pod.wave.co transcript/clip page for The Diary Of A CEO (Steven Bartlett)

quotes.text: They basically work to get my intracellular NAD to level age 16 equivalent.
quotes.source: NAD.com article quoting Bryan Johnson

NR.summary: Bryan Johnson currently includes NR as an active option in his Blueprint protocol and frames his support as self-experimentation, citing 90-day NR vs NMN trials that he says effectively raised his intracellular NAD to a much younger-equivalent level.

quotes.text: Resveratrol doesn't meet the evidence criteria to be included in the stack. We’re open to adjusting if that changes in the future.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com mirror of X post/reply, Apr 1, 2025

quotes.text: Resveratrol doesn't meet the evidence criteria to be included in the stack
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com mirror of X post/reply, Sep 27, 2024

quotes.text: resveratrol is not currently part of our protocol
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com mirror of X post/reply, Aug 30, 2023

quotes.text: Premium Extra Virgin Olive Oil is more powerful than resveratrol, NR, cold plunge, sauna and your favorite podcast. I consume 1 Tbsp (15 mL) w/ each daily meal.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com mirror of X post, Nov 8, 2023

resveratrol.summary: He is skeptical of resveratrol for his purposes and does not include it in his current Blueprint/Don’t Die stack because it does not meet his evidence criteria, though he is open to revisiting if evidence changes.

quotes.text: Exercise 6 hours a week.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

quotes.text: 3 sessions of strength training
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

quotes.text: 3 sessions of cardio
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

quotes.text: Aim for 150 minutes of moderate activity (Zone 2) during which you can maintain a conversation.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

quotes.text: And 75 minutes of vigorous exercise where you're unable to converse (like HIIT).
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

quotes.text: Daily exercise is one of the most powerful things you can do for health and longevity. Exercise is a power-law of health.
quotes.source: Blueprint exercise article ('Exercise and Fitness Protocol for Longevity')

quotes.text: My health protocol consists of: … + 1 hour of daily exercise
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com archived X/Twitter post

quotes.text: 6:00 am - 60-90 min workout: I focus on balance, flexibility, strength training and cardio.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page daily routine section ('Bryan Johnson's Protocol')

exercise.dosage_mentioned: 6 hours/week total; 3 strength sessions/week; 3 cardio sessions/week; 150 min/week moderate (Zone 2); 75 min/week vigorous (HIIT); 60–90 min morning workout block; ~1 hour daily exercise
exercise.summary: Bryan Johnson is a strong advocate of exercise for longevity and includes it as a core, currently active part of his Blueprint protocol with specific weekly and daily targets.

quotes.text: Calories: 2,250 (10% caloric restriction)
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page

quotes.text: My philosophy on nutrition is that every calorie must fight for its life.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page

quotes.text: My health protocol consists of: + 10% calorie restriction (2250 daily) …
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com tweet mirror

quotes.text: From: . caloric restriction 2000 cal (now 2250)
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com tweet mirror

quotes.text: Increased daily caloric intake to 2250 calories, up from 2000… 2250 calories is 10% Caloric Restriction …
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com tweet mirror

quotes.text: I was on a 20%… restriction… I’m now 2,250. So I’m 10 percent caloric restriction. … we saw no change in my markers …
quotes.source: Network State podcast transcript

quotes.text: the hypothesis was that caloric restriction is positive, but how much CR is optimal? We started with 20%. We had 1,950 calories… So, we went from 20% to 10%, and we lost nothing on the biomarkers.
quotes.source: Lifespan.io interview-style article

quotes.text: what I eat every day is I eat a diet of 2,250 calories. And the premise is that every calorie must fight for its life.
quotes.source: Podscripts transcript (Megyn Kelly Show episode page)

caloric restriction.dosage_mentioned: 10% caloric restriction; 2,250 calories/day (previously ~2,000/day; earlier ~1,950/day at ~20% restriction)
caloric restriction.summary: He actively practices and publicly advocates a moderate caloric restriction approach (currently ~10% at ~2,250 kcal/day), having tested deeper restriction and reduced it when biomarkers did not improve.

quotes.text: @Guygies @tomchapin you can get the spermidine via pills
quotes.source: X post (archived on posts.bryanjohnson.com), Aug 29, 2023

quotes.text: @algoflows @CryptoChaoss you can get the 13.5mg of spermidine via pills
quotes.source: X post (archived on posts.bryanjohnson.com), Jul 30, 2023

quotes.text: …spermidine, which he tells me stabilizes the DNA…
quotes.source: VICE interview writeup quoting Johnson, Jan 30, 2023

quotes.text: The “optimal amounts”… is 13.5 milligrams per day.
quotes.source: VICE interview writeup quoting Johnson, Jan 30, 2023

spermidine.summary: He actively uses and recommends spermidine (including citing 13.5 mg/day and pill form) and it remains included in the current Blueprint Essential Capsules product.

quotes.text: Today I started a next level age reversal intervention: taking Dasatinib, a Leukemia drug, and Quercetin, a plant flavonol. Together, they’re a senolytic.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post), May 2022

quotes.text: Protocol is Dasatinib 50mg + Quercetin 500mg daily for 3 consecutive days. 1x/mth for 6 months.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post), May 2022

quotes.text: I start this on Sunday: Dasatinib 50mg + Quercetin 500mg daily for 3 consecutive days. 1x/mth for 6 months.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post), May 2022

quotes.text: did a distatinib and quercetin cycle… it’s 50 milligrams of distatinib… and then 500 milligrams of quercetin. And you do it three days in a row for three months… [and] it dramatically increased my speed of aging.
quotes.source: Rich Roll Podcast transcript on podscripts.co, episode page (listed Jan 29, 2024)

quotes.text: we used dasatinib… and then quercetin… it’s a protocol where you do it three days for three months… and… my speed of aging spiked.
quotes.source: Network State Podcast transcript page, posted Jan 28, 2024

quotes.text: I have done two cycles of Dasatinib and Quercetin. We have some interesting data to share about this soon.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter reply), Jan 30, 2024

dasatinib quercetin.dosage_mentioned: Dasatinib 50 mg + Quercetin 500 mg daily for 3 consecutive days; described as 1x/month for 6 months (May 2022) and also as 3 days in a row for 3 months (Jan 2024 interviews).
dasatinib quercetin.summary: He experimented with dasatinib+quercetin senolytic cycles (50 mg/500 mg) but later reported his measured “speed of aging” worsened and it is not shown as part of his current publicly posted Blueprint protocol.

quotes.text: Senolytics eliminate senescent cells that accumulate with age and are mostly bad actors in the body.
quotes.source: X post (via Bryan Johnson tweet archive)

quotes.text: These interventions demonstrated a significant increase or acceleration of Biological Aging … 1. Pharmacological Interventions: Rapamycin …, senolytics.
quotes.source: X post/thread segment (via Bryan Johnson tweet archive)

quotes.text: Fisetin … 100 mg (per serving; serving size is 2 capsules).
quotes.source: Blueprint product page (Supplement Facts for Essential Capsules / Easy Stack context)

senolytics.summary: He endorses the senolytic concept and includes a senolytic-associated supplement (fisetin) in his current Blueprint stack, but expresses caution by citing data where pharmacological senolytics were associated with accelerated biological aging.

quotes.text: I take like some of the more interesting ones around aging, fisetin, luteolin, calcium alpha-ketoglutarate.
quotes.source: Dr. Mark Hyman podcast transcript (podscripts.co), 'The Science of Living Longer: Diet, Supplements, & Sleep | Bryan Johnson'

quotes.text: 24 supermolecules including … fisetin …
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules product page

quotes.text: Fisetin (Smoketree Extract) … An antioxidant from plants. Supports cellular health.
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules product page (ingredient description)

quotes.text: I’d tell people about the importance of extra virgin olive oil… spermidine… fisetin… luteolin…
quotes.source: Blueprint landing page (landing-page-social-1)

fisetin.dosage_mentioned: Take 2 capsules in the morning with food.
fisetin.summary: He publicly says he takes fisetin and Blueprint currently includes it in its Essential Capsules/Easy Stack, indicating active use and advocacy via inclusion in his protocol.

quotes.text: @jacobjig Agreed on Turmeric/curcumin. I'm unfamiliar with the bouncing exercises.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (Bryan Johnson X-post archive), reply to @jacobjig

quotes.text: What I do for routine blood glucose optimization: Metformin SR, Acarbose, Ginger. Cinnamon, Turmeric, Garlic, Zinc, D3, DHEA, + low GI/GL diet + CRON.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (Bryan Johnson X-post archive), blood glucose optimization thread

quotes.text: Bryan’s recommendation: Take with a shot of Blueprint EVOO
quotes.source: Blueprint product page (NAC + Ginger + Curcumin)

quotes.text: Curcumin extract std. 85% Curcuminoids (as) Curcuma longa 250 mg
quotes.source: Blueprint product page Supplement Facts (NAC + Ginger + Curcumin), per serving (3 capsules)

curcumin.dosage_mentioned: Curcumin extract (std. 85% curcuminoids) 250 mg per serving (3 capsules); directions: take 3 capsules daily with food
curcumin.summary: He is supportive of turmeric/curcumin and includes it in his current Blueprint stack via a curcumin-containing supplement (250 mg curcumin extract per daily serving).

quotes.text:
Not all Omega-3 is created equal
Consider getting your Omega 3 from algal or fish oils, plant oil sources are also good, but not as potent.
EPA and DHA are the bioactive Omega-3 fats essential for both brain and heart health.
quotes.source: X post (mirrored on posts.bryanjohnson.com)

quotes.text: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA/DPA 800 mg)
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page ('Bryan Johnson’s Protocol', 01.23.2026)

quotes.text: Reduce inflammation: omega-3s [Blueprint Omega-3 supplement coming soon] …
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post ('My sperm health protocol', 06.10.2025)

quotes.text: Blueprint Omega-3 contains 400mg EPA and 400mg DHA (800mg total).
quotes.source: Blueprint Omega-3 product page (FAQ)

quotes.text: Consume 2 vegan capsules morning or evening with food.
quotes.source: Blueprint Omega-3 product page (How to Use)

omega-3 fatty acids.dosage_mentioned: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA/DPA 800 mg); product: 400 mg EPA + 400 mg DHA (800 mg total) as 2 vegan capsules with food; (pregnancy guidance add-on: DHA/EPA 1000–2000 mg daily)
omega-3 fatty acids.summary: Bryan Johnson is pro–omega-3 (especially EPA/DHA), recommends algal or fish oil sources, and currently includes an 800 mg omega-3 dose in his active Blueprint protocol.

quotes.text: The active form of CoQ10 that supports mitochondrial energy production.
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules product page

quotes.text: Ubiquinol (as Reduced CoQ10) 50 mg
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules Supplement Facts panel

quotes.text: 36 supermolecules including … ubiquinol (CoQ10) …
quotes.source: Blueprint Longevity Mix + Essential Capsules (Easy Stack) product page

CoQ10.dosage_mentioned: Ubiquinol (as Reduced CoQ10) 50 mg per serving (2 capsules)
CoQ10.summary: CoQ10 (as ubiquinol) is actively included in Bryan Johnson’s current Blueprint stack and is positioned as supporting mitochondrial energy production, with 50 mg listed per serving in Essential Capsules.

quotes.text: Lithium (as Orotate) 1 mg
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules product page, Supplement Facts panel

quotes.text: Lithium Orotate — Why we chose it: A trace mineral that supports mood stability and cognitive balance.
quotes.source: Blueprint Essential Capsules product page, ingredient explainer

quotes.text: The Easy Stack includes lithium orotate among its “36 supermolecules.”
quotes.source: Blueprint homepage, Easy Stack section

quotes.text: Supports cognition with lithium orotate & full vitamin B-complex
quotes.source: Blueprint Easy Stack product page

lithium.dosage_mentioned: Lithium (as Orotate) 1 mg per serving (serving size: 2 capsules)
lithium.summary: Bryan Johnson/Blueprint currently includes low-dose lithium (as lithium orotate, 1 mg per serving) in the active Blueprint supplement stack and positions it as supporting mood stability, cognitive balance, and cognition.

quotes.text: In 2006, Shinya Yamanaka identified four transcription factors; Oct4, Sox2, Klf4, and c-Myc (OSKM), capable of resetting differentiated adult cells into a pluripotent, embryonic-like state.
quotes.source: X post archive (@bryan_johnson), Aug 31 2025 thread (posts.bryanjohnson.com tweets page 45)

quotes.text: But what if we can all go beyond this lucky number? What gives hope: •Cellular Reprogramming •Rapamycin + other metabolic modulators •Reversing senescence
quotes.source: X post archive (@bryan_johnson), Aug 26 2024

quotes.text: This is big. OpenAI and Retro used a custom model to make cellular reprogramming into stem cells ~50× better, faster, and safer.
quotes.source: X post archive (@bryan_johnson), Aug 31 2025 thread (posts.bryanjohnson.com tweets page 16)

quotes.text: He describes “clocks” that “look at DNA methylation patterns … patterns about your age,” and says he tested multiple clocks and “reversed my epigenetic age by 5.1 years.”
quotes.source: Podcast transcript paraphrase (First Principles #15 transcript page)

epigenetic reprogramming.summary: He publicly promotes cellular/epigenetic reprogramming (including OSKM/Yamanaka factors) as a promising longevity pathway, but there is no sourced evidence here that he currently uses any in-human reprogramming intervention in his Blueprint protocol.

quotes.text: Epigenetic clocks are one option to track progress towards longevity escape velocity… A test of your DNA Methylation can reveal how fast or slow you are aging. My most recent result was .57, meaning I age one year over 21 months instead of 12.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archive of @bryan_johnson on X)

quotes.text: I continuously trace my epigenetic biological age using the PACE (DunedinPACE) clock, … this clock is superior in both reliability and sensitivity, especially in detecting shifts in biological age in healthy cohorts…
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archive of @bryan_johnson on X)

quotes.text: Their clock uses 9 cpg sites and saliva rather than blood. Mine = 6 validated epigenetic clocks ie horvath, levine etc, that use thousands of cpg sites.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archive of @bryan_johnson on X)

quotes.text: Three epigenetic clocks were used… i. PHENOAge… ii. GrimAge… iii. DunedinPACE…
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archive of @bryan_johnson on X)

epigenetic clocks.summary: He actively uses epigenetic clocks—especially DunedinPACE—as a preferred, ongoing way to measure speed of aging and track progress, while emphasizing higher-signal, validated multi-clock approaches over weaker clocks.

quotes.text: A lot of buzz around ozempic (Semaglutide). Diet, exercise, smoking cessation and alcohol moderation can extend health/lifespan 10,000 days, whereas Semaglutide may have negligible or even negative effect on health/lifespan.
quotes.source: Bryan Johnson X post archive, Dec 11, 2022

quotes.text: To evaluate the potential benefits I will start a 12 week trial of the GLP-1/GIP RA Tirzepatide (Commercial name: Munjaro for diabetes, Zepbound for weight loss).
quotes.source: Bryan Johnson X post archive, Dec 19, 2024

quotes.text: I’ll start with a weekly dose of 0.5 mg, …
quotes.source: Bryan Johnson X post archive, Dec 19, 2024

quotes.text: Data is not there yet to call Ozempic as anti-aging therapy,
quotes.source: Benzinga article quoting Johnson's X reply, Aug 6, 2025

GLP-1 agonists.dosage_mentioned: tirzepatide 0.5 mg weekly (starting dose) for a 12-week trial
GLP-1 agonists.summary: He is skeptical of Ozempic/semaglutide as an anti-aging/lifespan intervention but has shown willingness to self-experiment via a time-limited tirzepatide (GLP-1/GIP) trial, while GLP-1 drugs are not listed in his current public protocol Rx stack.

quotes.text: Discontinuing therapy: completed 6, 1L young plasma exchanges. 1x/mo (1 w/ my son). Evaluated biomarkers from biofluids, devices and imaging, no benefits detected.
quotes.source: Archived X post, Jul 5, 2023

quotes.text: Young plasma exchange may be beneficial for biologically older populations or certain conditions. Does not in my case stack benefit on top of my existing interventions. Alternative methods of plasma exchange or young plasma fractions hold promise.
quotes.source: Archived X post, Jul 5, 2023

quotes.text: I am no longer injecting my son's blood. I've upgraded to something else: total plasma exchange.
quotes.source: Archived X post, Jan 28, 2025

quotes.text: Steps: 1. Take out all blood from body 2. Separate plasma from blood 3. Replace plasma with 5% albumin & IVIG
quotes.source: Archived X post, Jan 28, 2025

quotes.text: TPE removes all of my body’s plasma and replaces it with Albumin. The therapy objectives are to remove toxins from my body. The evidence is emergent.
quotes.source: NDTV article quoting his X wording (secondary report)

young plasma.dosage_mentioned: Young plasma: 6 exchanges of 1L, 1x/month (one with his son). TPE replacement: 5% albumin & IVIG (no volume/frequency stated).
young plasma.summary: He discontinued young-donor plasma exchanges in July 2023 after detecting no biomarker benefits, and later shifted to total plasma exchange (albumin/IVIG replacement) as an alternative plasma-based intervention.

quotes.text: Hyperbaric oxygen therapy ranks as one of the highest value health therapies I've done.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page, Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: Conditions: 100% oxygen at 2 atmospheric pressures.
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page, Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: Schedule: 60 sessions, 5 sessions per week, duration: 90 min (20 min followed by 5 min air break, repeat).
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page, Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: Extended my telomere length by 2.6% (10.3  11.4 kb).
quotes.source: Blueprint protocol page, Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: I've started a new longevity protocol: hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT).
quotes.source: Posts mirror of X/Twitter post, Nov 25, 2024

quotes.text: We completed a fairly extensive literature search and concluded there is supporting evidence for its usage for acute therapies but there was insufficient evidence to use it for our objective of biological aging.
quotes.source: Posts mirror of X/Twitter post, May 4, 2023

quotes.text: The evidence for telomere elongation and senescent cells didnt cross our thresholds to implement into our protocol.
quotes.source: Posts mirror of X/Twitter post, May 4, 2023

quotes.text: HBOT ranks as one of the highest value health therapies Ive done.
quotes.source: Posts thread page (mirror of X/Twitter), Mar 18, 2025

quotes.text: My HBOT protocol in a glance +  + 2 ATA + 90 minute sessions + 20 min breathing 100% oxygen. 5 min break. Repeat. + 60 sessions within 90 days.
quotes.source: Posts page listing (mirror of X/Twitter), Mar 18, 2025

quotes.text: Limitations While doing HBOT I also underwent three total plasma exchanges (TPE).
quotes.source: Posts page listing (mirror of X/Twitter), Mar 18, 2025

hyperbaric oxygen therapy.dosage_mentioned: 100% oxygen at 2 ATA; 90 min sessions with 20 min O2 then 5 min air break (repeat); 60 sessions total; 5 sessions/week (noted as within ~90 days in 2025 post)
hyperbaric oxygen therapy.summary: Bryan Johnson currently includes HBOT in Blueprint and describes it as one of the highest-value therapies he has done, after initially saying (in 2023) evidence was insufficient for his biological-aging objective.

quotes.text: I frequently get asked if I cold-plunge. Aside from the one time I tried it with my friend Steve Aoki, cold plunging is not a part of my normal routine.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post ('Do I cold plunge? The answer is no. Here’s why'), Nov 29, 2024

quotes.text: Following Blueprint has me metabolically cold plunging 24/7.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post ('Do I cold plunge? The answer is no. Here’s why'), Nov 29, 2024

quotes.text: A regular cold plunge at 55°F for 60 min only decreases temp by 0.83°F
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post ('Do I cold plunge? The answer is no. Here’s why'), Nov 29, 2024

quotes.text: I keep getting cooler … Waking body temp: 2020: 98.7°F 2024: 93.4°F (34.1°C) … I’m cold plunging 24/7.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com archived X post

quotes.text: @PunkOnChain … I haven't added any cold therapy to the protocol yet.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com archived X reply, Jun 19, 2025 (per provided research)

quotes.text: @aeyakovenko I've not done a cold protocol but am open to it
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com archived X reply

quotes.text: Last week I learned why people do cold exposure. I was jet lagged & sleep deprived. Did cryotherapy for 3 min at -112°C. Immediately had a clean coffee high; wiping out my sluggishness. Doing it before when well rested, I couldn't feel any difference.
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com archived X post (appears on page 507)

cold exposure.dosage_mentioned: Cold plunge example: 55°F for 60 min; Cryotherapy: 3 min at -112°C
cold exposure.summary: He does not currently include cold plunging/cold therapy in his Blueprint routine, has only tried it in limited one-off contexts, and remains open to it while emphasizing his regimen already produces a 'metabolically cold' state.

quotes.text: My sauna protocol: Type: Dry sauna … Temperature: 200°F (93°C) … Frequency: daily, 7 days a week … Duration: 20 minute sessions … Protection: Ice pack applied to the groin area … Rehydration: 36 oz mineral supplemented water …
quotes.source: Blueprint blog, 'Bryan Johnson’s Protocol', Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: Safe Activation of Heat Shock Proteins… a modest 1°C increase in core temperature is sufficient to activate these proteins without the risk of hyperthermia.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog, 'Bryan Johnson’s Protocol', Jan 23, 2026

quotes.text: There’s evidence that sauna is a potential detox and longevity intervention… We’re going to put it to the test.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog, 'Is Sauna Worth the Hype?', May 23, 2025

quotes.text: Repairing and maintaining proteins (heat shock proteins)
quotes.source: Blueprint blog, 'Is Sauna Worth the Hype?', May 23, 2025

quotes.text: My sauna protocol: … 200 °F (93° C) … daily, 7 days a week … 20-minute sessions … Ice pack applied to the groin … Rehydration: 36 oz mineral-supplemented water …
quotes.source: Blueprint blog, 'Is Sauna Worth the Hype?', May 23, 2025

quotes.text: My sauna protocol: + Type: Dry sauna … + Temperature: 200 °F (93° C) + Frequency: daily, 7 days a week + Duration: 20-minute sessions + Protection: Ice pack applied to the groin area … + Rehydration: 36 oz …
quotes.source: Bryan Johnson Posts archive (embeds X post text), May 7, 2025

quotes.text: At last, we tested sauna for longevity. I’m dropping my sauna protocol and explaining why they are definitely worth the hype.
quotes.source: Apple Podcasts episode description, 'How Sauna Detoxified My Body', Jun 26, 2025

sauna.dosage_mentioned: Dry sauna at 200°F (93°C) for 20 minutes daily (7 days/week); ice pack applied to groin; rehydration with 36 oz mineral-supplemented water.
sauna.summary: Johnson strongly advocates sauna as a longevity intervention and includes a daily 200°F/20-minute dry sauna protocol in his current Blueprint routine, citing heat shock protein activation and experiment-driven results.

quotes.text: “A study published yesterday in Cell Reports showed Ketogenic Diet (high in fat and low in carbs and proteins) can reverse autoimmunity in a mouse model for multiple sclerosis MS.”
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post)

quotes.text: “Note: A lot of people want to label others into nutrition camps that war with each other i.e. plant based, carnivore, keto, paleo, etc.. I don’t identify with any of them. I follow the scientific evidence and data in determining what I eat.”
quotes.source: Blueprint Protocol page

quotes.text: “People on Blueprint are carnivores, omnivores, keto, paleo and plant based. Everyone is welcome.”
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post, page 193)

quotes.text: “Among lean, metabolically healthy individuals on a ketogenic diet who exhibit elevated LDL-C and ApoB but have optimal triglyceride and HDL levels (i.e., the LMHR phenotype), …”
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com (archived X/Twitter post referencing LMHR; text truncated on archive page)

ketogenic diet.summary: He discusses ketogenic diet research and keto-related lipid phenotypes but explicitly avoids identifying with “keto” as a nutrition camp, and his current Blueprint macros are not ketogenic.

quotes.text: In a cutting-edge therapy I had 300 million stem cells injected into my shoulders, hips, and joints. The aim: joints to match my total bone mineral density, which is in the top 99.8% for 30-year-olds.
quotes.source: X post mirrored on posts.bryanjohnson.com

quotes.text: I had 300 million young Swedish bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells injected into my shoulders, hips, and joints.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post

quotes.text: I had 300 million bone marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) injected into six joints over two days: knees, shoulders, and hips.
quotes.source: Blueprint blog post

quotes.text: Yeah, I did 100 million Mesenchymal stem cells of young Swedish bone marrow… People have used these Mesenchymal stem cells for Long COVID… for heart conditions… for reducing inflammation. I’ve used it for joint therapies, regenerating joints… we did it as a general rejuvenation therapy.
quotes.source: Third Wave podcast transcript (Episode 231 page)

quotes.text: That’s probably because my health biomarkers are already in the top 1%… So it’s pretty difficult for my subjective feeling of wellness to improve.
quotes.source: Business Insider interview quoting him

stem cell therapy.dosage_mentioned: 300 million bone marrow-derived MSCs injected into six joints over two days (knees, shoulders, hips); also mentions 100 million MSCs (young Swedish bone marrow) in interview
stem cell therapy.summary: Bryan Johnson has personally tried mesenchymal stem cell joint injections as a measured, clinical-trial-style experimental rejuvenation intervention, but does not present it as a standard ongoing Blueprint protocol component.

quotes.text: “We saw the same thing when I did, human growth hormone to regenerate my thymus. So I did, HGH for a hundred days, and we, according to MRI, we did in fact, rejuvenate my thymus by seven years… That also dramatically increased my speed of aging. And then it came back down after I had discontinued the HGH.”
quotes.source: Network State Podcast transcript page (ns.com), Bryan Johnson episode

quotes.text: “I’d say the worst outcome was using human growth hormone. We were repeating a study that was done for thymus rejuvenation… We did 100 days of human growth hormone… So according to three MRI’s we regenerated my thymus by 7 years… Plus, I had intracranial pressure increase, I had my blood glucose were messed up.”
quotes.source: LinkedIn reposted transcript of a clip (secondary hosting)

quotes.text: “New Blueprint therapy… Three peptides for thymus rejuvenation… Intramuscular injection, 10 mg daily for 5 days every 6 months.”
quotes.source: Archived X-post mirror on posts.bryanjohnson.com

quotes.text: “...we just completed the thymus for the first time… getting a high quality image of the thymus to age it to work on rejuvenation was very hard…”
quotes.source: Kayla Barnes-Lentz podcast transcript page

thymus regeneration.dosage_mentioned: Peptides: intramuscular injection, 10 mg daily for 5 days every 6 months; HGH: used for 100 days (dose not clearly stated in primary source); Metformin: 500 mg, 6-week cycles on/off (not stated as thymus-specific)
thymus regeneration.summary: He experimented with thymus regeneration (notably a 100-day HGH trial he discontinued due to adverse effects and faster aging), while remaining engaged in thymus rejuvenation efforts via imaging and an experimental peptide-based protocol rather than an ongoing TRIIM-style regimen.

quotes.text: Aspirin is valid for preventing inflammation, although typical doses are higher than the typical baby aspirin dose of 80-100, even such low dose can have some anti-inflammatory and anti-inflammaging effect. You have to beware of side-effects including potential gastric ulcer, …

My urine is now blue.

I’m experimenting with methylene blue.

quotes.text:
My methylene blue protocol
+ 25 mg/day of methylene blue orally
+ 5 days a week
+ Keeping a 2-day weekly clearance break to prevent accumulation, especially in the brain
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com post (tweet mirror), Aug 11, 2025

quotes.text:
Methylene blue safety checklist
+ G6PD first: Get a G6PD blood test, skip methylene blue if deficient.
+ Med review: Do not take methylene blue with any antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs), or other serotonergic supplements/herbs or to linezolid (an antibiotic).
+ Hydrate
quotes.source: posts.bryanjohnson.com post (tweet mirror), Aug 11, 2025

quotes.text: According to my telomerase activity I am 12 years old … I just took my first Q-TRAP test, which measures the relative telomerase activity in blood cells, my relative telomerase activity is 7.7%. 2% or less and your telomere's are in decline.

quotes.text: Today I started a next level age reversal intervention: taking Dasatinib, a Leukemia drug, and Quercetin, a plant flavonol. Together, they’re a senolytic.

quotes.text: “NR vs NMN? I used each for 90 days & both were effective in getting my intracellular NAD levels to age 16 equivalent, 31 yr age reversal as semi-indirect bioage marker. Recent levels 54.6μM via Jinfiniti test. Used @truniagen NR & @JinfinitiPM NMN.”
